reasoning: The investigation began with the root cause ranked list, which strongly identified the A1 interface as the primary source of the anomaly, with a1_packet_loss_dl_percentage exhibiting the lowest distance to the failure node. Following the O-RAN hierarchy, a failure on the A1 interface (Non-RT RIC -> Near-RT RIC) logically propagates to the Near-RT RIC, which then affects its downstream communication with the O-DU/O-CU via the E2 interface. The subsequent anomalies observed on the F1-u and F1-c interfaces are treated as symptomatic noise resulting from the instability of the O-CU and O-DU caused by the degraded E2 control loop.
actionable_steps: 1. Inspect the physical and virtual network links between the Non-RT RIC and Near-RT RIC. 2. Verify the health and configuration of the Non-RT RIC's transmission buffers and network interface cards. 3. Analyze A1 interface traffic for congestion or misconfigured Quality of Service (QoS) policies that may be causing packet drops.
